// SLIDE 01 — HOOK

CHAPTER 12 — FIGMA AS A MACHINE-READABLE SPECIFICATION

The distinction between human and machine consumers is structural, not cosmetic.

Chapter 12 — Figma as a Machine-Readable — A human documentation consumer wants compression. The canonical usage, not every variant value. Usage guidance, not a co
NARRATION

The distinction between human and machine consumers is structural, not cosmetic. A human documentation consumer wants compression. The canonical usage, not every variant value. Usage guidance, not a complete enumeration of layout constraints. The example that covers 80 percent of use cases, not the edge cases. Compression makes documentation scannable. A human who needs more detail can open the Figma file directly.

// SLIDE 02 — WHAT THE FIGMA API PROVIDES

WHAT THE FIGMA API PROVIDES.

The Figma REST API exposes more than most practitioners use. The documentation sync in Chapter 10 needed names, descript

What the Figma API Provides — From GET /v1/files/:key , at the component level: id (the node ID, stable within a file version but not across structura
NARRATION

The Figma REST API exposes more than most practitioners use. The documentation sync in Chapter 10 needed names, descriptions, and variant properties. A machine-readable specification needs everything the API can provide. From GET /v1/files/:key , at the component level: id (the node ID, stable within a file version but not across structural refactors); key (the component library key, more stable than node ID across file changes); componentSetId (the parent set, if this component is a variant); variantProperties (the exact key-value map of variant dimensions and values for this

The Component Specification Schema —
NARRATION

A schema is a contract. Before writing build-spec.mjs, define what downstream tools will consume.

// SLIDE 04 — BUILD-SPEC.MJS

BUILD-SPEC.MJS

const CODE_CONNECT = process.argv.find(a => a.startsWith('--code-connect='))?.split('=') — null;
if (!TOKEN — !FILE_KEY) {
const wait = parseInt(res.headers.get('Retry-After') — '30', 10);
for (const of Object.entries(fileData.styles — {})) {
NARRATION

build-spec.mjs import { writeFileSync, readFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync } from 'fs'; import { join } from 'path';

// SLIDE 05 — THE TOKEN ALIAS CHAIN: THE NON

THE TOKEN ALIAS CHAIN: THE NON-ENTERPRISE PATH

The alias chain — tracing from a raw fill value back through a style reference to a design token — is where the machine-

The Token Alias Chain: The Non-Enterpris — The full alias chain requires the Variables API, which is gated behind the Enterprise plan . Three alternatives exist fo
NARRATION

The alias chain , tracing from a raw fill value back through a style reference to a design token , is where the machine-readable spec most clearly outperforms human documentation. A human reader can infer that the primary button's blue fill probably comes from the brand-primary token. A code generator cannot infer this. It must be told. The full alias chain requires the Variables API, which is gated behind the Enterprise plan . Three alternatives exist for other plan tiers.

// SLIDE 06 — W3C DTCG AS THE INTERCHANGE FO

W3C DTCG AS THE INTERCHANGE FORMAT.

The component spec JSON above is specific to this toolchain. When specs need to cross tool boundaries — different teams,

W3C DTCG as the Interchange Format — DTCG defines a JSON format for token data where $value, $type, and $description are the primitive fields:
NARRATION

The component spec JSON above is specific to this toolchain. When specs need to cross tool boundaries , different teams, different code generators, different platforms , the W3C Design Tokens Community Group (DTCG) format is the emerging standard for token interchange. DTCG defines a JSON format for token data where $value, $type, and $description are the primitive fields:

// SLIDE 07 — CONTRACT TESTS

CONTRACT TESTS

Missing nodeId — Hard fail — exit 1
Missing key — Hard fail — exit 1
Missing specVersion — Hard fail — exit 1
Missing Code Connect mapping — Warn — exit 0
NARRATION

A machine-readable spec is only useful if it is complete. Contract tests are the mechanism that prevents it from silently regressing. test-spec-contract.mjs Run after build-spec.mjs

// SLIDE 08 — HOW THIS FEEDS MCP AND THE AI

HOW THIS FEEDS MCP AND THE AI CODING AGENT

The component spec JSON from build-spec.mjs is the structured context that makes an AI coding agent's output trustworthy

How This Feeds MCP and the AI Coding Age — With the spec, the agent knows the exact variant dimensions and values for each component, so it can generate a TypeScri
NARRATION

The component spec JSON from build-spec.mjs is the structured context that makes an AI coding agent's output trustworthy. Without it, an agent working in an MCP session has to infer component structure from what is visible in the Figma canvas , richer than nothing but structurally incomplete. With the spec, the agent knows the exact variant dimensions and values for each component, so it can generate a TypeScript props type without guessing.

// SLIDE 09 — FAILURE MODES

FAILURE MODES

The spec is large.→Node IDs are not stable across file restructuring.→Variable data requires Enterprise.→Code Connect must be maintained separately.→Internal structure depth.
NARRATION

The spec is large. A design system with 500 components and deep node trees produces a spec file several megabytes in size. This is not a problem for a CLI writing to disk. It is a problem for an AI coding agent loading the entire spec into context. Use the per-component files and load only what is relevant to the current task. Node IDs are not stable across file restructuring. When a component is moved to a different page or rebuilt, its node ID changes.

// SLIDE 10 — DECISION RULES

DECISION RULES

Generate the spec whenever the Figma library is published, a new component is added, or variant properties change.

Decision Rules — Use build-spec.mjs output as the source for code generators, AI coding agent context, design-to-code pipeline inputs, an
NARRATION

Generate the spec whenever the Figma library is published, a new component is added, or variant properties change. Use build-spec.mjs output as the source for code generators, AI coding agent context, design-to-code pipeline inputs, and component scaffolding CLIs.

// SLIDE 11 — WHAT COMES NEXT

WHAT COMES NEXT.

The spec is the structured context the next chapter needs. Chapter 13 connects the design system file to an AI coding ag

NARRATION

The spec is the structured context the next chapter needs. Chapter 13 connects the design system file to an AI coding agent via the Figma MCP server, using the spec as the foundation that makes the agent's output trustworthy. An agent that knows the variant dimensions, the token aliases, and the import paths can generate code that matches the design system. An agent that has only the canvas has to guess at all three.

// SLIDE 12 — THESIS

THE CORE CLAIM.

The spec is the structured context the next chapter needs. Chapter 13 connects the design system file to an AI coding ag

NARRATION

The spec is the structured context the next chapter needs. Chapter 13 connects the design system file to an AI coding agent via the Figma MCP server, using the spec as the foundation that makes the agent's output trustworthy. An agent that knows the variant dimensions, the token aliases, and the import paths can generate code that matches the design system. An agent that has only the canvas has to guess at all three.
